surface_verification.runner: n/a (Semgrep not installable on the Windows dev box)
surface_verification.justification: Comment-only + .semgrepignore change with no runtime surface. The behavioral effect (Semgrep findings drop) is the CI scanner's output, not locally runnable on this Windows dev box (semgrep core binary). Empirical verification = the PR's own security.yml Semgrep scan + the GitHub code-scanning open-alert count (130 -> ~0), confirmed at the Delivery Watch BEFORE merge; local guards (tsc/oxlint/unit) confirm no behavior change.

behaviors.behavior: The 8 inline nosemgrep comment insertions + ternary/assignment-adjacent comments do not break TypeScript compilation
The comment insertions introduce no new lint errors
behaviors.evidence: server oxlint exit 0 + client oxlint exit 0 — only pre-existing warnings in unrelated files; none on edited lines

behaviors.behavior: Touched core modules (parameter-resolver, actions-schema-validator, cli-compat) behave identically — comments only, bidi-defense regex untouched
behaviors.evidence: 62/62 server unit tests pass across 5 files; git diff shows the bidi CONTROL_CHAR_REGEX line is unchanged context (only comment lines added above it)

behaviors.behavior: .semgrepignore + nosemgrep eliminate the 130 Semgrep false positives (spawn-shell-true 105, regexp/bidi/websocket residue) without removing SAST on production code
behaviors.evidence: CI: the PR's security.yml Semgrep scan SARIF + GitHub code-scanning open-alert count drop from 130 toward ~0 — verified at Delivery Watch before merge (the merge is gated on this confirmation)
